content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 26, 1976

Diane Lee
Office of the City Attorney
151 West Mission St.
San Jose, CA 95110

Dear Ms. Lee:

This letter confirms our recent telephone conversation concerning gifts received by the dependent
child of an elected officer. On a recent trip, the
mayor of San Jose flew first class. Two children,
one of them a dependent child, accompanied her. The
children had regular coach tickets, but the airline
permitted both children to sit in the first class
section with their mother. On the basis of these
facts, the benefit received by the children when
they were permitted to sit in the first class section
of the airplane is not a reportable gift to the
parent. I have previously sent you an opinion setting forth certain standards to determine when gifts
received by the spouse or dependent children of an
elected official should be considered to be gifts to
the official. Opinion requested by Ken Cory, 2 FPPC
Ops. 47 (No. 75-094-A, April 22, 1976). Under the
three part test established in the Cory opinion, the
benefit received by the children in the present case
is not a gift to the parent.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West
Staff Counsel

NEW:plh

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5901 • ENFORCEMENT/CONFLICT OF INTEREST 322-6441
